title: Four-Bar Linkage
goal: Transforms crank rotation into controlled oscillatory output and coupler trajectories for motion design.
gallery_summary: Create swinging, back-and-forth, or circular motion through four connected bars.
parts: 4 bar-shaped links forming one closed chain
Ground Link: Fixed base connecting two pivot points
Input Link (Crank): Rotating driver link that provides input motion
Coupler: Floating link connecting input and output
Output Link (Rocker): Driven link that produces output motion
advantages: Low friction and good energy efficiency
disadvantages: Can reach singular or dead-point positions
materials: Rigid link bars with low-backlash joints
Precision bearings or bushings at pivots
Fasteners and pins sized for cyclic loading
cautions: Full rotation capability is required before assembly (check Warnings).
Verify clearances to avoid link collisions
Evaluate transmission quality near extreme positions
